stories.title: Trump threatens Iran's power plants, bridges. And, Artemis II readies for lunar flyby
stories.description: Trump threatened to bomb Iran's power plants and bridges unless it opens the Strait of Hormuz. And, NASA's Artemis II crew prepares to make its closest approach to the moon.

stories.title: Iran pushes back against Trump's deadline
stories.description: Iran's top officials pushed back against President Trump's deadline to open the Strait of Hormuz, striking a defiant tone as the warring sides traded missile attacks.

stories.title: US airman rescued after his fighter jet was shot down in Iran
stories.description: NPR's Tom Bowman reports on the race to rescue a U.S. airman after his fighter jet was shot down.

stories.title: Trump unleashes curse-filled social media rant at Iran after U.S. rescues colonel
stories.description: In a profanity-laden post on Truth Social, President Trump lashed out at Iran and injected new volatility into the conflict, hours after U.S. forces carried out a high-risk rescue mission.

stories.title: Trump issues expletive-laden threat to Iran over Hormuz Strait blockage
stories.description: The US president says he will destroy Iranian power plants and bridges if the vital waterway is not reopened.

stories.title: How rescue of US airman in remote part of Iran unfolded
stories.description: The operation to extract him from the ground in hostile territory was hugely complex and involved multiple US government agencies.

stories.title: Trump declares victory after rescue but threats to US operation in Iran still loom
stories.description: The rescue could impact how Trump views a ground operation to take Kharg Island or to seize enriched uranium sites.

stories.title: 'We are at the edge of a battlefield': BBC reports near Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: The BBC's Orla Guerin travels to the edge of the critical waterway that Iran has put a stranglehold on.

stories.title: US says it has arrested relatives of late Iranian ​general Qasem ​Soleimani
stories.description: The niece and grand-niece of Qasem Soleimani are in the custody of US Immigration and Customs Enforcement, officials said.

stories.title: French-owned ship passes through Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: It appears to be the first ship owned by a major European firm to go through the strait since the conflict began.

stories.title: Experts dispute US account of deadly Iran sports hall strike in Lamerd
stories.description: Six weapons experts have contested the US claim that video evidence suggests an Iranian missile could have hit the hall.

stories.title: International law experts allege violations in Iran war
stories.description: The White House says it is making the entire region safer by eliminating short and long-term threats.

stories.title: 'This has got me worried': Iranians fear what comes next after US strike on Karaj bridge
stories.description: President Trump has warned Iran of strikes on bridges and electric power plants if its leaders do not agree to his terms to end the war.

stories.title: Iran's focus on survival means same regime still firmly in place
stories.description: Tehran is showing resilience despite Trump's claims of regime change, writes BBC Persian editor Amir Azimi.

stories.title: 'I haven't slept for days': Iranians describe mounting desperation after a month of war
stories.description: Ordinary people from various parts of Iran describe expanding strikes, economic pain and fear of repression.

stories.title: When will the Iran war end? Tracing the Trump administration's timelines
stories.description: Experts say many US presidents have offered a timeline for a conflict - only to then shift their estimates.

stories.title: Ros Atkins on…the cost of the Iran war
stories.description: The BBC's Analysis Editor Ros Atkins takes a closer look at US costs as it continues its war with with Iran.

stories.title: Trump leaves key questions unanswered as he seeks to calm nerves over Iran war
stories.description: There were some glaring omissions in the US president's primetime address, writes the BBC's Gary O'Donoghue.

stories.title: Israel's perpetual war with Iran may be hard to win with military might alone
stories.description: The war with Iran has not yet resolved any of Israel's regional conflicts in the way its prime minister suggested it would.

stories.title: US president Donald Trump warns Tehran to reopen Hormuz or face "hell"
stories.description: Iran and the United States received a draft proposal late Sunday calling for a 45-day ceasefire and the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: War in the Middle East: Donald Trump threatens to hit Iran power plants
stories.description: Israeli strikes killed the intelligence chief of Iran's Revolutionary Guards, as the Islamic republic on Monday defied threats from US President Donald Trump to devastate civilian infrastructure if it does not reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Could China's adoption of electric vehicles shield it from the energy crisis?
stories.description: As crude prices hover around the $110 per barrel mark on Monday morning, consumers worldwide are feeling the impact of the war in Iran and the closure of the Hormuz Strait when they head to the petrol pump. In China, where more than half of new car sales are for electric vehicles...

stories.title: 'Hit by both sides': US war on Iran threatens Iraq's 'careful strategy of multi-alignment'
stories.description: Catherine Viette is pleased to welcome Dr Renad Mansour, Deputy Director for the Middle East and North Africa Programme and Director of the Iraq Initiative at Chatham House. For years, Iraq has pursued a strategy of multi-alignment, explains Dr. Mansour, carefully maintaining rel...

stories.title: Middle East war: ‘No indication that legitimate US-Iran talks taking place’
stories.description: Reporting from Tehran, FRANCE 24’s Reza Sayad said that “everyone” in Iran is aware of US President Donald Trump’s deadline to reopen the strait of Hormuz, but regardless of what the White House might be saying “there’s no indication that legitimate talks are taking place” betwee...

stories.title: Middle East war live: Israeli strikes hit Iran's largest petrochemical plant, defence minister says
stories.description: Several explosions were heard coming from Iran's largest gas complex at Asaluyeh in Iran's south, the country’s Fars news agency reported on Monday. News of the blasts came after the chief of the Revolutionary Guards’ intelligence ‌organisation, Majid Khademi, was killed in US-Is...

stories.title: Trump hails victory after US mission to rescue pilot from Iran
stories.description: American commandos deployed deep into Iranian territory to rescue a downed airman, US news outlets reported on Sunday, hours after President Donald Trump announced that the crew member had been recovered "safe and sound."

stories.title: War in the Middle East: Israel says residential building hit by Iranian missile
stories.description: 'Stuck in a warzone': France 24's Noga Tarnopolsky describes the plight of Israelis who are unable to evacuate from the north.

stories.title: Israel launches new strikes on Lebanon, forces closure of Syria border crossing
stories.description: Israeli airstrikes on Beirut and southern Lebanon on Sunday killed at least 11 people, including a family of six, and wounded dozens more. The attacks also prompted Syria to temporarily close its main border crossing with Lebanon, as Israel's conflict with Hezbollah intensifies.

stories.title: Has Trump confirmed Iran’s claim that protesters were US-armed?
stories.description: US president says Washington armed Kurdish Iranian opposition groups during the January demonstrations.

stories.title: Iran threatens Bab al-Mandeb closure: How would that affect world trade?
stories.description: If the chokepoint were shut, along with the Strait of Hormuz, a quarter of the world's energy supply would be blocked.

stories.title: Pakistan offers ‘two-phased’ truce deal to end US-Israel war on Iran
stories.description: Iran, while still reviewing the proposal, says it won't reopen Hormuz as part of a temporary ceasefire.

stories.title: Trump’s ‘Bridge Day’ threat: Can a last-ditch ceasefire plan work?
stories.description: Mediators Pakistan, Turkiye and Egypt are proposing a 45-day ceasefire plan. But Iran is reluctant.

stories.title: Yemen’s civilians fear fallout after Houthis enter Iran war
stories.description: Fears of air strikes and rising prices dominate everyday life in Sanaa as Yemen becomes embroiled in regional conflict.

stories.title: Trump says US armed Iranian dissidents via Kurds, Kurdish groups deny claim
stories.description: Trump's admission gives credence to Iranian assertion that the January protests were backed by the West to create chaos.

stories.title: Iran’s top university bombed as US, Israel intensify attacks; 34 killed
stories.description: Tehran says it will respond 'in kind' to attacks on its infrastructure, accuses Trump of inciting 'war crimes'.

stories.title: Iran war: What is happening on day 38 of US-Israeli attacks?
stories.description: Tensions continue after Trump's threat on Truth Social for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: A new regional order for the Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: The GCC and Iran can come up with a new security architecture for the Strait of Hormuz that can give Trump an off-ramp.

stories.title: Vietnam’s gig workers slammed by rising fuel costs amid fallout of Iran war
stories.description: Diesel prices have more than doubled in the Southeast Asian nation amid Iran's blockade of the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Iran war live: Tehran says response to ceasefire proposals formulated
stories.description: Iran says no direct talks during escalating US-Israeli attacks as Trump's Hormuz deadline nears.

stories.title: US Democratic lawmakers visit Cuba, call on Trump to ‘bring the rhetoric down’
stories.description: Two Democrats from the US House of Representatives visited Cuba last week, the first such delegation to go to the island this year since US President Donald Trump imposed a de facto oil blockade in a bid to bring Cuba’s communist-run government to its knees. Another Israeli attack on the Jnah neighbourhood in Beirut killed four people and injured 39 others...

stories.description: United States President Donald Trump said on Sunday that US forces had safely recovered a second airman downed in Iran, calling it “one of the most daring search and rescue operations in US history”. stories.description: WARSAW: The European Union should end sanctions on Russian oil and gas imports, take steps to restore Druzhba oil pipeline flows and end the war in Ukraine to tackle the energy crisis stemming from the war in Iran, Slovak Prime Minister Robert Fico said on Saturday. stories.description: WASHINGTON: US President Donald Trump is considering a bro­ader cabinet shake-up in the wake of Attorney General Pam Bondi’s removal this week, as he grows increasingly frustrated with the political fallout from the war with Iran, five people familiar with internal White House di...
